titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the African American Television Scripts
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 1 published digital object. And that object are comprised of 43 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 28, 2024 at 00:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from George Robert Minkoff Inc. on the Adele Gutman Nathan Theatrical Collection Fund, 1999.
July 2015 acquisition: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2015.
June 2017 acquisition [1 of 2]: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.
June 2017 acquisition [2 of 2]: Purchased from Royal Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.
June 2021 acquisition: Purchased from Royal Books on the James Weldon Johnson Memorial Collection of Arts & Letters Fund, 2021.
May 2024 acquisition: Purchased from Clouds Hill Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2024.
August 2024 acquisition: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc., on the Bradford F. and Lila L. Swan Fund, 2024.
September 2024 acquisition [1 of 2]: Purchased from Clouds Hill Books on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 2024.
September 2024 acquisition [2 of 2]: Purchased from Clouds Hill Books on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine groupings: October 1999 acquisition; July 2015 acquisition; June 2017 acquisition [1 of 2]; June 2017 acquisition [2 of 2]; June 2021 acquisition; May 2024 acquisition; August 2024 acquisition; September 2024 acquisition [1 of 2]; September 2024 acquisition [2 of 2].

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: African American Television Scripts. James Weldon Johnson Memorial Collection in the Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization.

The finding aid for this collection was compiled from a list supplied by the bookseller at the time of acquisition, with some further editing carried out in 2011. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are based on that list. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. The description of the papers is also drawn largely from information supplied by the bookseller.
Former call number: Uncat JWJ MS 65.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of scripts and a small number of photographs documenting television programs featuring African American actors, entertainers, and topics from the 1950s through the 1990s. While some material from an earlier period of television broadcasting is present, including Amos and Andy (1952), Buelah (1950-1953), and The Jack Benny Show (1964), scripts for shows that aired in the 1970s and 1980s predominate. Sanford and Son (1974-1976), The Jeffersons (1975-1978), Good Times (1974-1976), Diff'rent Strokes (1978-1986), Webster (1983-1989), and The Cosby Show (1984-1991), situation comedies depicting African American familes in varying social conditions, are particularly well represented. In addition to long-running programs, the collection includes scripts for variety specials, miniseries, and pilots such as Guess Who's Coming to Dinner (1975) and Skin Game (1974). The collection also includes additional production materials, including call sheets, correspondence, ratings, and research materials, as well as a file documenting the Association of Black Motion Picture and Television Producers, including meeting minutes and copies of outgoing correspondence.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The African American Television Scripts is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from George Robert Minkoff Inc. on the Adele Gutman Nathan Theatrical Collection Fund, 1999.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2015.

did.unittitle: "2nd Annual Soul Train Music Awards" script

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes scripts for Diff'rent Strokes and related papers including correspondence, ratings, and production materials.

did.unittitle: Beauty's in the eye of Arnold
Beauty is in the eye of Arnold
Beauty is in the eye of Arnold
It's my party and I'll cry if I want to
So you want to be a rock and roll star
Lifestyles of the poor and unknown

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains an untitled manuscript by Gary Coleman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes scripts for Webster and related papers including production materials, a photograph, posters, and other papers.

did.unittitle: It's a dog eats dog world
Man in the red flannel suit
San Francisco parts I' II and III
San Francisco, parts I' II and III
Yippee, Yay, I'm going to HEAVEN
Doing time at the community center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains Dudley for President, Vote 4 Dudley posters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes synopsis for Katherine the brave and parental guidence suggested episodes and general season story synopsis.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Royal Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

"The Cat's in the Cradle" first mimeo updated

"Faith, Hope, and Charity" - Part One first mimeo

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Royal Books on the James Weldon Johnson Memorial Collection of Arts & Letters Fund, 2021.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: From the collection of A. Dudley Johnson, Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition consists of televisions scripts, production materials, and other papers relating to Diff'rent Strokes and Webster.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: File contains black and white photograpjs from the 1977 broadcast, a rerun from 1978 and a later broadcast on a local New York City television station.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains a full run of the series scripts including variant drafts.

did.unittitle: Show #1, by William Blinn and Ernest Kinoy, second draft
Show #2, by Ernest Kinoy and William Blinn
Show #3, by James Lee and William Blinn
Show #4, by James Lee and William Blinn
Show #5A, by M. Charles Cohen, second draft
Show #6, by James Lee and William Blinn
Show #7, by M. Charles Cohen, revised second draft
Show #7, by M. Charles Cohen, revised second draft
Show #8, by M. Charles Cohen, final draft

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Cloud Hill Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this accession are arranged in chronological order of draft date.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains seventy-five original production scripts and drafts of the television show A Different World .

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc., on the Bradford F. and Lila L. Swan Fund, 2024.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains scripts, photographs, and a poster from the television mini-series "Roots".
